CLAUDE.md

Source: CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

ebirdst — project instructions for Claude

This file is local-only (gitignored) and layers on top of the global R style guide in ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md. Follow both; this file adds project-specific workflow requirements.

Overview

ebirdst is an R package (CRAN + GitHub) for downloading and analyzing the eBird Status and Trends Data Products from the Cornell Lab of Ornithology. It does not fit models — it is a client for accessing pre-computed data products (rasters, tabular estimates, range polygons) and a toolkit for loading, subsetting, visualizing, and post-processing them. There are two distinct product families with separate version years (see ebirdst_version()): Status (weekly relative abundance, occurrence, count, PIs, PPMs, ranges) and Trends (per-year population change, a subset of species/seasons).

Commands

Prefer devtools::load_all() while iterating (not library(ebirdst)).

  • Run one test file: devtools::test_file("tests/testthat/test-loading.R")
  • Run full suite: devtools::test()
  • Re-document after roxygen edits: devtools::document()
  • Full package check: devtools::check()
  • Format / lint (scoped to R/ by config): air format R/ and jarl check R/ (autofix: jarl check --fix R/)
  • Full release checklist (vignettes, pkgdown, win-builder): see makefile.R — release time only, not routine changes.

Tests and vignettes require the "yebsap-example" dataset; tests/testthat/ setup.R downloads it once into a temp EBIRDST_DATA_DIR for the whole suite.

Architecture

The package is organized by pipeline stage rather than by product. Key files in R/ and how they fit together:

  • access-key.R — stores/retrieves the Status & Trends access key via rappdirs config (set_ebirdst_access_key()); the "*-example" datasets bypass the key requirement.
  • download.R — the entry point (ebirdst_download_status(), ebirdst_download_trends(), ebirdst_download_data_coverage()). Downloads are laid out on disk as <data_dir>/<version_year>/<species_code>/.... This fixed layout is load-bearing: every load_*() function reconstructs paths from it, so renaming/moving downloaded files breaks loading. download_* flags plus a pattern regex control which files are fetched; some files are mandatory and always downloaded.
  • load.R (largest file) — the read layer. load_raster() returns terra SpatRaster stacks (52 weekly layers, resolutions like "27km"/"3km"); other loaders return tabular data (load_pis, load_pds, load_ppm, load_regional_stats, load_config) or sf objects (load_ranges). load_config() / load_fac_map_parameters() read per-species JSON that drives plotting (custom projection, legend bins/labels).
  • sample.R — spatiotemporal subsampling of point data (grid_sample(), grid_sample_stratified(), assign_to_grid()) used to reduce spatial bias before analysis; not tied to a specific data product.
  • trends.R — post-processing of Trends tabular data into rasters/vectors (rasterize_trends(), vectorize_trends()) and unit conversions.
  • manage.R — local data inventory and cleanup (ebirdst_data_inventory() and its print.ebirdst_inventory S3 method, ebirdst_delete()).
  • ebirdst-palettes.R — Status-specific color palettes for maps.
  • utils.R — internal validators (is_flag/is_integer/is_count), get_species() (resolves common/scientific name or code to a species code), date_to_st_week().
  • data.R — documents the three bundled datasets in data/: ebirdst_runs (the authoritative species list, seasons, quality ratings, trends availability), ebirdst_predictors, and ebirdst_predictor_descriptions.
  • ebirdst-deprecated.R / ebirdst-defunct.R — the version-migration surface; API changes land here rather than silently breaking callers.
  • zzz.R — .onAttach prints the active Status/Trends version years and citations.

Species are referenced throughout by six-letter eBird species code (e.g. "woothr"), but user-facing functions accept common or scientific names and resolve them via get_species() against ebirdst_runs.

Formatting and linting — always run these

  • After writing or editing any file in R/, run air format R/. The repo’s air.toml scopes formatting to R/ only (data-raw/, examples/, tests/, and makefile.R are intentionally excluded), so air format . is also safe to run from the repo root.
  • After writing or editing any file in R/, run jarl check R/ (or jarl check . — jarl.toml restricts it to R/ regardless). Fix obvious/auto-fixable issues with jarl check --fix R/. For warnings that require judgment (e.g. internal_function on a ::: call with no public alternative), use judgment rather than blindly forcing a fix.
  • Do this on every change, not just when explicitly asked to format or lint.
  • Never run air/jarl over tests/, data-raw/, examples/, or makefile.R — those are intentionally out of scope per air.toml / jarl.toml.

Tests

  • Every new exported or internal function needs an accompanying test in tests/testthat/test-{name}.R (see the global CLAUDE.md for naming and structure conventions). Don’t skip this because a change feels small.
  • When modifying an existing function’s behavior, update or extend its existing tests rather than leaving them stale.
  • Run the affected test file(s) with devtools::test_file() before running the full suite; run devtools::test() before considering a change done.
  • Use the "yebsap-example" example dataset for integration tests — it’s already downloaded by tests/testthat/setup.R. Don’t add tests that require downloading real species data.

Documentation

  • After changing any roxygen2 comment, re-run devtools::document() (this regenerates NAMESPACE and man/*.Rd). Never hand-edit NAMESPACE or files under man/.
  • If a function’s @export tag is missing or misplaced, that’s a real bug (it silently breaks the public API) — not a style nitpick.

Package development workflow

  • Bump the version in DESCRIPTION and add a bullet to NEWS.md for any user-facing change (new function, changed argument, bug fix affecting output).
  • Prefer devtools::load_all() over library(ebirdst)/install.packages() when iterating locally.
  • Before considering larger changes complete, run devtools::check() and resolve new NOTEs/WARNINGs/ERRORs it introduces (see makefile.R for the fuller release checklist — vignettes, pkgdown site, win-builder checks — which is only needed at release time, not for routine changes).
  • Vignettes only execute locally (BUILD_VIGNETTES=TRUE), never during R CMD check — don’t try to make them run in CI.

Git and GitHub

  • This repo is typically contributed to via fork + upstream remote (see CONTRIBUTING.md): changes land on a branch, then a PR against ebird/ebirdst.
  • You have permission to run git and gh (including gh pr create) directly. Still follow the general git safety protocol: create new commits rather than amending, never force-push main, never skip hooks unless explicitly asked, and confirm before anything destructive (reset --hard, force-push, branch deletion) even though the command itself doesn’t require a prompt.

On this page

Developed by Matthew Strimas-Mackey, Shawn Ligocki, Tom Auer, Daniel Fink.
